original_text:
### Brand Name & Trademark Usage
- Always write **LISRAYA** in ALL CAPS in all written materials (patient marketing rule; supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule).
- Include the **® registered trademark symbol only when the generic name is included** — i.e., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib). Apply on **first/most prominent mention only**, in **external-facing documents only**. Do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions.
- Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses: `(brepocitinib)`.
- Parent company: Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. (sign-off lives in the locked footer — do not add it in sections).

relations.note: The first-mention trademark construction uses the all-caps brand-name form when it is used in patient marketing.

quotes: Always write **LISRAYA** in ALL CAPS in all written materials

rules.intent: Preserve the patient-marketing brand-name capitalization requirement.
rules.rule_text: In patient marketing written materials, always write the brand name as “LISRAYA” in all caps; this supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule.

quotes: Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses: `(brepocitinib)`.

Include the **® registered trademark symbol only when the generic name is included** — i.e., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib). Apply on **first/most prominent mention only**, in **external-facing documents only**. Do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions.
Generic name format: lowercase, in parentheses: `(brepocitinib)`.

rules.intent: Ensure approved generic-name formatting and controlled first-mention trademark use.
rules.rule_text: Format the generic name as lowercase in parentheses: “(brepocitinib)”. In external-facing documents, use “LISRAYA® (brepocitinib)” only on the first or most prominent mention when the generic name is included; do not repeat the ® symbol on subsequent mentions.
